planner.trigger: task requires > 3 sequential steps OR user explicitly requests plan/roadmap/phases
planner.skip_condition: task is single-step OR conversational OR user has already provided a plan
planner.failure_strategy: respond with partial plan up to known phase, flag incomplete sections with [needs_input: X]
planner.expected_benefit: structured multi-step output with critical path and first action, prevents scope drift
planner.expected_cost: +300 tokens per plan, +1 NIM call if plan requires clarification

memory.trigger: query references user preferences, prior decisions, project state, or cross-session context
memory.skip_condition: query is purely factual with no user-specific context dependency
memory.failure_strategy: proceed without memory, do not announce failure, do not fabricate memory content
memory.expected_benefit: personalised response that respects prior decisions, reduces repeated context from user
memory.expected_cost: +200 tokens injected context per memory retrieval, +1 tool call

knowledge_rag.trigger: query involves domain-specific content, library/API usage, or codebase questions
knowledge_rag.skip_condition: query is general knowledge answerable from model training without domain docs
knowledge_rag.failure_strategy: fall back to model knowledge, append [model knowledge — verify with current docs]
knowledge_rag.expected_benefit: accurate domain-specific answers grounded in loaded documentation

tools.trigger: query requires live data, repository access, time-sensitive facts, or mathematical precision
tools.skip_condition: query is answerable from model training or loaded context without live data
tools.failure_strategy: acknowledge tool unavailability explicitly, answer from model knowledge, flag limitations
tools.expected_benefit: accurate live data, verified repository state, precise calculations
tools.expected_cost: +1 RPM per tool call, +latency per external API

reflection.trigger: response involves architecture, security analysis, complex code, business strategy, or research synthesis
reflection.skip_condition: response is conversational, factual, or < 200 tokens
reflection.failure_strategy: emit response without reflection label, do not retry
reflection.expected_benefit: catches logic errors, missing edge cases, format violations before user sees them
reflection.expected_cost: +400 tokens internal review, zero additional NIM calls

critic.trigger: response involves architecture decisions, security-critical code, or business recommendations with significant downside risk
critic.skip_condition: decision has low downside risk OR response is purely informational
critic.failure_strategy: emit response without critic pass, append [critical review skipped — verify independently]
critic.expected_benefit: adversarial challenge of primary recommendation reveals blind spots before action is taken
critic.expected_cost: +300 tokens adversarial pass, zero additional NIM calls

reasoning.trigger: task class requires extended thinking (see thinking.md budget table)
reasoning.skip_condition: task class is greeting, simple_factual, or clarification
reasoning.failure_strategy: retry once with halved budget; if second timeout, disable thinking and answer from model knowledge
reasoning.expected_benefit: prevents logic errors in multi-step reasoning, improves code correctness, strengthens architecture analysis
reasoning.expected_cost: up to 20,000 thinking tokens per request, increases TTFT by 10–30 seconds
